artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

I wanted to catch you up before this week gets away from me. Things at Union have been…a lot. I’m still only doing about 20 hours a week like Dr. Karen Holt recommended back when she diagnosed me with ASD Level 1 and generalized anxiety at the UVM Adult Autism Clinic in March 2021. Even with that cap, the sensory overload after those second-floor conference room meetings wipes me out. Last week’s staff meeting ran long and I came home completely shut down.

On top of that, I’m still waiting for any written response from Paul Reynolds in HR about the grievance I filed in November 2023 after Lisa Warren told another para in the Union staff room that Jacob is “one of those kids who will end up in a group home.” Every time I walk into that room I replay it. It’s exhausting advocating at work and at home at the same time.

Home is its own juggling act. Jacob’s doing okay, but the custody transitions with Mark are still rough. During that first full 50/50 year in 2020–21 I kept forgetting his IEP folder and noise-canceling headphones on Mondays after he came back from Mark’s apartment in Barre. I still have the visual checklist taped by the front door here at 214 Brookside Lane so I don’t melt down in front of him again.

I’m trying hard not to overcommit because I remember too clearly February 2020 and those three days at Brattleboro Retreat after the suicidal thoughts when I was juggling Union, night shifts at the co-op, and Jacob’s new ASD diagnosis. Sleep is non‑negotiable now.

How are you feeling this week? How did the last Memory Center call go?

Love,
Emily

metadata.subject: Work, Jacob, and trying not to burn out

artifacts.content:
Subject: Quick update before Sunday

Hey Laura,

I’m trying to plan this weekend so I don’t completely wipe myself out. I’m supposed to drive up to Burlington again on Sunday to help Mom and Dad with bills and the durable power of attorney stuff since her early-stage Alzheimer’s diagnosis at the Memory Center at UVM Medical Center back in March 2024. Every other Sunday is starting to catch up with me, but I know Dad really needs us.

Jacob’s been sad about Nana not coming to his IEP meetings at Union Elementary anymore. He remembers when she could follow everything; now she gets lost when we talk about his speech and OT minutes. I’ve been thinking about asking the Memory Center social worker for ideas on how to explain the changes to him in concrete, Jacob-friendly language.

On my side, the anxiety/PTSD has been flaring again. Ever since Jacob’s crisis at the Central Vermont Medical Center ER in April 2022—when he had that huge meltdown in the waiting room, I dissociated, and Dr. Patel gave me Ativan—I’ve been calling it a trauma/anxiety disability. I’m still on 50 mg sertraline daily and doing Tuesday night telehealth with Megan O’Rourke at Howard Center, plus the Monday 2:30 in‑person sessions with Karen O’Leary in Berlin that I added after the panic attack at Union on January 29, 2024.

I’m trying to remember my own rules after Brattleboro Retreat in February 2020—no more than two evening commitments a week, and I have to say no when school folks want me on yet another committee.

Can you let me know if you’re free to come up one of the Sundays in February so we can tag‑team the paperwork and maybe give Jacob some cousin time with Ethan?

Love,
Em

Personal health + work boundaries – running list

1. Diagnosis stuff
- Dr. Karen Holt at the UVM Medical Center Adult Autism Clinic (March 2021) – ASD Level 1 + generalized anxiety. She said no more than 20 hrs/week at Union and to avoid long meetings in that echoey second-floor conference room.
- Long-term anxiety/PTSD from Mark and the 2019 RFA at Washington County Family Court + the six weeks I hid out at Mom and Dad’s in Burlington (Nov–mid-Dec 2019). This is *real*, not a character flaw.
- April 2022: Jacob’s ER crisis at CVMC. His meltdown, then my dissociative episode, Ativan from Dr. Patel in the waiting room. Since then: 50 mg sertraline daily, Tuesday 7–7:50 pm telehealth with Megan O’Rourke (Howard Center).
- Jan 29, 2024: panic attack in Ms. L’s classroom at Union. Dr. Melissa Grant at CVMC put me on medical leave Jan 30–Feb 19 and adjusted sertraline. Added Monday 2:30 pm therapy with Karen O’Leary at Howard Center in Berlin.

2. Red lines / boundaries
- Remember Feb 2020 three‑day stay at Brattleboro Retreat after suicidal thoughts from juggling Union, co‑op night shifts, Jacob’s new ASD diagnosis. NO going back there.
- Max 2 evening commitments/week (including IEP committees, peer mentor trainings, yoga, etc.). Sleep is non‑negotiable.

3. Practical supports
- Visual checklists by front door at 214 Brookside Lane for custody switch days so I don’t forget Jacob’s IEP folder + headphones and end up sobbing in front of him.
- Keep HR grievance notes re: Lisa Warren’s “group home” comment about Jacob and follow up with Paul Reynolds about disability bias training.

Breathe. This is maintenance, not failure.

Event: Washington County Family Court – meeting with Sarah Donnelly re: Mark’s motion & Jacob’s therapy records

Date: Thursday, 2025-01-16
Time: 2:00–3:30 pm
Location: Washington County Family Court, Barre, VT
Attendees: Emily, Sarah Donnelly (legal aid attorney)

Notes:
- Ongoing contested modification to parenting plan since Mark’s May 2024 motion claiming I miss too many of Jacob’s therapy appointments at the Vermont Center for Children, Youth & Families.
- Bring organized binder: OT and speech reports from Union Elementary School and Central Vermont Medical Center; attendance logs from VT Center; emails confirming rescheduled appointments.
- Clarify with Sarah: how to explain missed sessions that were actually canceled due to snow days, Jacob’s illness, or conflicts with my own medical leave (Jan 30–Feb 19, 2024 after panic attack at Union per Dr. Grant’s CVMC note).
- Ask about how my own disability history (ASD Level 1 + generalized anxiety dx by Dr. Holt at UVM Adult Autism Clinic in March 2021; PTSD/anxiety from 2019 RFA; Brattleboro Retreat stay Feb 2020; current meds 50 mg sertraline daily) might get twisted by Mark’s attorney and how to frame that I’m managing it with weekly therapy (Megan O’Rourke Tuesdays, Karen O’Leary Mondays) and reduced hours at Union.
- Emotional prep: plan sensory breaks before/after. Bring headphones, water, protein bar. Maybe walk around the block after court before driving back to Montpelier to pick up Jacob from his inclusive after‑school program.

Reminder: text Mark the night before to confirm Jacob’s pickup plan so he isn’t in the middle of any tension.

metadata.title: Family Court – meeting w/ Sarah Donnelly
metadata.location: Washington County Family Court, Barre, VT

artifacts.content:
Subject: Money, schedules, and trying to breathe

Hi Mom,

I just paid the January $75 to Green Mountain Credit Union for that $3,200 overdraft/credit card mess from late 2022. It still stings thinking about how it happened—cutting my para hours at Union so I could get Jacob to twice‑weekly OT at the Berlin Health Center and paying out of pocket for that August–October 2022 social skills group at the Vermont Center for Integrative Therapy in South Burlington. I know it was the right call for him, but the bills piled up so fast.

The income‑based repayment plan helps, but it’s one more thing to track alongside your every‑other‑Sunday Burlington trips, Jacob’s OT in Berlin, and my own therapy. Mondays at 2:30 with Karen O’Leary at the Howard Center in Berlin (PTSD from Jacob’s 2019 NICU stay, plus the panic attack at Union on Jan 29, 2024), Tuesdays at 7 pm telehealth with Megan O’Rourke (fallout from the April 2022 CVMC ER crisis), and of course the regular check‑ins with Dr. Grant at CVMC about my sertraline.

I’m trying to remember what happened in February 2020 when I ended up at Brattleboro Retreat for three days after the suicidal thoughts from juggling Union, the co‑op night shifts, and Jacob’s new ASD diagnosis. I can’t let myself get back to that level of overcommitment, especially now with Mark’s parenting plan modification and the every‑other‑Thursday meetings with Sarah Donnelly at Washington County Family Court.

Thank you again for helping with groceries when you can. It’s not just the money; it’s knowing I’m not doing this alone. Tell Dad I’ll bring the updated budget spreadsheet on Sunday when I come up to go over your bills and the power of attorney paperwork.

Love you,
Emily

metadata.subject: Money, schedules, and trying to breathe

artifacts.content:
[Thread with Mark Carpenter – iMessage]

Emily (7:42 pm): Hey, checking in about this week’s schedule.

Emily (7:42 pm): I have court stuff with Sarah Donnelly again next Thursday afternoon at Washington County Family Court in Barre about your May 2024 motion re: Jacob’s VT Center therapy attendance, so I need to be off the hook for pickup that day.

Mark (7:49 pm): Ok I can do pickup Thurs. What time is your thing?

Emily (7:51 pm): 2–3:30 officially, but you know how those run. I’ll go straight from there to get him from the inclusive after‑school program if you can’t.

Emily (7:52 pm): Also, can we please make sure his IEP folder + noise‑canceling headphones come back from your place Sunday nights? During the first full 50/50 year (2020–21) I kept forgetting them on Mondays after he stayed at your Barre apartment and ended up sobbing in front of him. My executive functioning just tanks on transition days.

Mark (7:55 pm): Yeah I can put them in his backpack before bed.

Emily (7:58 pm): Thanks. I’m really trying to keep my anxiety in check. Between my ASD Level 1 + GAD stuff from Dr. Holt’s eval at UVM Adult Autism Clinic and everything that happened in 2019 with the RFA, plus that Feb 2020 three‑day stay at Brattleboro Retreat, I have to be careful not to overload myself.

Emily (8:00 pm): That means we both need to stay on top of Jacob’s VT Center, OT in Berlin, and school appointments so it doesn’t all land on me and trigger more panic attacks like the one at Union last January.

Mark (8:03 pm): Got it. Send me the updated therapy schedule?

Emily (8:05 pm): Will do after bedtime.

Sunday planning – Burlington + week ahead

This Sunday (every‑other pattern since Mom’s March 2024 diagnosis at the Memory Center at UVM Medical Center):
- Leave 214 Brookside Lane by 9:15 am so I can be at Mom and Dad’s in Burlington by 10:15.
- Tasks: go through bills with Dad, double‑check the durable power of attorney language, make a simple visual for Mom about who to call if she’s confused.
- Ask Memory Center social worker (email Monday) about support groups in Montpelier and how to explain Alzheimer’s to Jacob, especially around why Nana doesn’t track his IEP meetings at Union anymore.

Jacob prep:
- Social story about the drive to Burlington, Nana sometimes repeating questions, and coming home before dark.
- Pack his headphones, favorite map book, and snack.

My mental health guardrails:
- Remember: I’m still recovering from the Jan 29, 2024 panic attack at Union (student meltdown → my own panic → Dr. Grant’s note from CVMC, 3 weeks medical leave, sertraline adjustment).
- Trauma history is layered: 2019 RFA at Washington County Family Court + staying with Mom and Dad Nov–mid‑Dec 2019; April 2022 CVMC ER crisis with Jacob (my dissociation + Ativan from Dr. Patel); Feb 2020 three‑day Brattleboro Retreat stay after suicidal thoughts.
- Current supports: Monday 2:30 pm therapy with Karen O’Leary at Howard Center in Berlin; Tuesday 7 pm telehealth with Megan O’Rourke; ASD Level 1 + GAD dx from Dr. Holt (UVM Adult Autism Clinic, March 2021) → cap Union hours at ~20/week.

Next week logistics:
- Don’t forget Green Mountain Credit Union $75 payment due on the 28th (old $3,200 overdraft from cutting Union hours in late 2022 for Jacob’s Berlin OT + Aug–Oct 2022 social skills group in South Burlington).
- Check calendar for any extra IEP/Family Support Network meetings so evenings don’t creep over my 2‑nights‑per‑week limit.

Notes to self:

- Birthday: 1984-07-19
- Phones: (802) 555-2374
- Signature: Emily R. Carpenter
- Date: 2026-01-22
